content.explanation: Python programs make decisions with if/elif/else, and repeat work with loops. A 'for' loop walks through each item in a list one at a time, and an 'if' inside it lets you act only on the items that meet some condition — this combination is the backbone of almost every automation script.

check.statement: Fill in the blank: to only print interfaces whose status is NOT 'up', what goes in 'if iface["status"] ____ "up":'?

solution.explanation: != is Python's 'not equal to' operator — 'if iface["status"] != "up":' runs the block only for interfaces whose status is anything other than 'up', which is exactly what you want when hunting for down interfaces.
